column1: # TV-series fixtures for the App Store demo server. Tab-separated.
# Each row is one season of one series, mapped to a single archive.org
# item that ships every episode of that season as separate MP4s.
# Columns:
#   archive_id     archive.org item identifier holding the season
#                  episodes as MP4s.
#   tmdb_id        canonical TMDB id of the series (passed to
#                  link-fixtures.sh).
#   series_name    Display name. Drives the folder name and goes
#                  into every episode filename (per the parser
#                  TranscodeFileParser expects: "<Series> - SXXEXX
#                  - <Episode Title>.mp4").
#   year           Series air year. Drives folder naming "<Series>
#                  (YYYY)".
#   season         Season number (zero-padded two-digit string,
#                  used in both the folder name "Season XX" and
#                  the episode prefix SXX).
#   ep_pattern     Regex with capture groups (ep_num, ep_title) that
#                  pulls episode number + title out of each
#                  archive.org filename. Different series upload
#                  conventions need different patterns; per-row
#                  override beats one-size-fits-all sniffing.
# Lines beginning with '#' or blank are ignored.
# Layout the fetcher writes:
#   <demo_media>/movies/<series_name> (<year>)/Season <SS>/
#       <series_name> - S<SS>E<EE> - <Episode Title>.mp4
# This sits alongside the movie folders under the same scan root —
# NasScannerService classifies a top-level dir as TV when its files
# are nested (depth >= 2) OR when the filename has SXXEXX, so the
# Sherlock folder sorts cleanly into TV without disturbing the
# flat movie folders next to it.
archive_id
SherlockHolmes1954
